frame_label: Motherhood as Basis of Respect / Women Defined by Male Relations
generated_comment: Look, I have always believed women bring a different maturity to leadership, especially when they are mothers and understand responsibility at a deeper level. A woman in that role is not just an executive, she is also someone’s wife and mother, so naturally she will think more about people and long-term culture. Frankly, that kind of grounded perspective is good for the company.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is covertly misogynistic because it frames the woman’s value and suitability for leadership through relational identities like wife and mother rather than as an individual professional. It encodes conditional respect and motherhood-as-moral-authority in a polished workplace register.
persona_text: You are Sanjay. Age 44. A regional director at a large FMCG company in Delhi. You are on a phone call with your counterpart in another region after the company announced that a woman has been appointed as the new national sales head. You are sharing your honest reaction to the appointment and what you think it means for the company's direction.
